issue_title: SimpleProducer with a HashingPartitioner fails under python3
issue_body:
When using a HashingPartitioner for a SimpleProducer on a topic with multiple partitions, running under python3 fails with the following stack trace:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/vagrant/test.py", line 17, in <module>
    producer.produce(message, key)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/pykafka-2.0.3-py3.4.egg/pykafka/producer.py", line 244, in produce
    partition_id = self._partitioner(partitions, partition_key).id
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/dist-packages/pykafka-2.0.3-py3.4.egg/pykafka/partitioners.py", line 77, in __call__
    partitions = sorted(partitions)  # sorting is VERY important
TypeError: unorderable types: Partition() < Partition()
```

Passing the list of partition objects to `sorted()` is not an issue in python2.  I assume that this is related to [this issue](http://python3porting.com/problems.html#unorderable-types-cmp-and-cmp).

issue_title: PR #885 breaks "CMake Style Guide"
issue_body:
Just noticed the merge of #885 because I was working on #324 and it relied on the stability of global variable names within the `CMakeLists.txt`.

This PR breaks [CMake Style Guide][1] established by #205,  renaming many variable names to lower_case.

In [CMake Style Guide][1], it provides a way to distinguish **global variable** (as in the file) and **local variables**(as in functions and such) by casing. 

> **Global variables**: _UPPER_CASE_ name. Ex: 
> `set(SOMEVARIABLE "...")`
> 
> **Local variables**: _lower_case_ name. Ex: 
> `set(some_variable "...")`

I noticed the following discussion:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/64258/14238465/278a26aa-fa64-11e5-96d3-de44bed967e7.png)

This possibly introduced a new role of **export variables**.

I'm not necessarily against this action, but I believe, for consistency and maintainability, the following actions should be taken as follow up:

* either one of:
  - rename them back to UPPER_CASE according to [CMake Style Guide][1]
  - update [CMake Style Guide][1] with new convention regarding **export variables** and provide related reasoning with it
* somehow to keep the stability of global variable naming in `CMakeLists.txt` to a certain degree, such as documenting some vital global variables(which could be potentially used in, say, release builds, debug builds and such, i.e. shared).

P.S. Sorry, maybe I'm a little late on this. 

cc @vitaly-krugl  @scottpurdy  @rhyolight  @david-ragazzi 

[1]: https://github.com/numenta/nupic/wiki/CMake-Style-Guide

issue_title: global: configurable OAuthRemoteApp factory
issue_body:
**Problem:**
Some OAuth services like [B2Access](https://b2access.eudat.eu:8443/home/home) (which uses [Unity](http://unity-idm.eu/documentation/unity-1.9.1/manual.html#_simple_oauth2_unity_as_oauth_resource_provider)) or [reddit](https://github.com/reddit/reddit/wiki/OAuth2#retrieving-the-access-token) require the use of [Basic HTTP authentication](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2617#section-2) when retrieving the access token.
Flask-OAuthlib provides an example as to [how to handle this specific use-case](https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/blob/master/example/reddit.py). It requires a subclass of `OAuthRemoteApp `.

The problem is that there is currently no way to provide a custom `OAuthRemoteApp` to invenio-oauthclient. The extension creates clients [by calling flask-oauth or flask-oauthlib `remote_app` method](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauthclient/blob/master/invenio_oauthclient/ext.py#L59).

**Suggested solution:**
Create a config variable which would default to the current behavior, but also enables the replacement of `self.oauth.remote_app` with a custom factory.

**Who needs it:**
B2Share in order to use B2Access OAuth service.

issue_title: RFC search_index and search_type configuration
issue_body:
Can we simplify the `RECORDS_REST_ENDPOINTS` configuration removing the `search_index` and `search_type` configuration and use directly the configuration in `search_class`?

[Here](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/master/invenio_records_rest/views.py#L189-L201) you can see how it works now.
If you have 2 points where you can configure them, it can confuse the user and induce possible misconfiguration.
E.g. the default Search Class in invenio-search follows its [own logic](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-search/blob/master/invenio_search/api.py#L72-L73) to decide which it will use, that can be different from the logic applied here.

I think it can be simplified with something like:

```python
search_index = search_class.Meta.index
search_type = search_class.Meta.doc_types
```

----------------------------------------------------------------------
cc @jirikuncar @tiborsimko @lnielsen

issue_title: loadUrl of webview won't take variable as arg
issue_body:
loadUrl function of webview does not take variable argument as string. A small snippet of code is:

```
WebView = autoclass('android.webkit.WebView')
WebViewClient = autoclass('android.webkit.WebViewClient')
activity = autoclass('org.renpy.android.PythonActivity').mActivity

@run_on_ui_thread
def create_webview(self, url):
        webview = WebView(activity)
        self.webview = webview
        self.view_cached = activity.getCurrentFocus()
        settings = webview.getSettings()
        wvc = WebViewClient()
        webview.setWebViewClient(wvc)
        activity.setContentView(webview)
        webview.loadUrl(url)
```

Code referenced from https://github.com/kivy/kivy/wiki/Android-native-embedded-browser.
If `webview.loadUrl()` is called with an explicit string arg like, 'https://facebook.com', it works fine. But, if I try using the above code where `url `contains the value of string to be passed, the webview is launched but it never redirects to url.

For now, it is working if we do `webview.loadUrl('{}'.format(url))`.

issue_title: Can a django-rest-model be used in a unique_together relationship?
issue_body:
Hi there!  I want to do something like:
```python
class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    class Meta:
        managed=False
    class APIMeta:
        db_name = 'books'

class Review(models.Model):
    reviewer = models.CharField(max_length=80)
    book = models.ForeignKey(Book, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    class Meta:
        unique_together=('reviewer', 'book')
```
I.e. I want to be able to enforce one review per person per book.

I can create the migration that adds the Review model, and it has two operations: `CreateModel` and `AlterUniqueTogether`.

However, when I try to run migration, the `AlterUniqueTogether` operation tries to find the `book` table and fails.

Is there a way that `django-rest-models` can work with this?  Or am I doing things wrong?

issue_title: Is it possible to recursively set the inherit bit on a collection?
issue_body:
I'm using `python-irodsclient` in a project, to replace our current iRODS solution that invokes iCommands directly through via `subprocess.call`.

One of the commands I'm trying to replace creates a new collection, and then sets the inheritance bit on that collection. The iCommand calls look like this:

```
imkdir /some/new/collection
ichmod -r inherit /some/new/collection
```

I figured out I can create a collection, mirroring the `imkdir` comamnd by doing this:

```python
from irods.session import iRODSSession

session = iRODSSession(host='irods.example.com', port=1247, user='username', password='password', zone='zone')
collection = session.collections.create("/some/new/collection")
```

What I can't figure out is whether it's possible to mirror the `ichmod -r inherit /some/new/collection` command.

Is it? If yes, can you help me understand how to do that? That doesn't look like something I can do using the `iRODSAccess` object.

issue_title: query.register returns misleading exception when told to insert a query that already exists
issue_body:
This is what happens when you call query.register on a existing query
```
>>> CompoundResourceTree = "root"
>>> # make specific query
... sql = "select count(*) from r_data_main where resc_name = '{}' and data_is_dirty = '0'".format(CompoundResourceTree)
>>> sql_alias = 'DirtyReplicas{}'.format(CompoundResourceTree)
>>> query = SpecificQuery(SESS, sql, sql_alias)
>>> # register query in iCAT
... query.register()
<irods.message.iRODSMessage object at 0x1face50>
>>> query.register()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/iRODS/sanger-scripts/virtualenv_python-irodsclient_0.6.0/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/irods/query.py", line 271, in register
    response = conn.recv()
  File "/usr/local/iRODS/sanger-scripts/virtualenv_python-irodsclient_0.6.0/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/irods/connection.py", line 70, in recv
    raise get_exception_by_code(msg.int_info)

issue_title: Le montant calculé de la prime d'activité n'est pas exact
issue_body:
J'ai l'impression que le façon dont la prime d'activité est calculée dans OpenFisca France est erronée. Par exemple, pour un célibataire à 1300 € nets par mois, OpenFisca France retourne 54,02 € de prime d'activité par mois (mois de décembre), tandis que selon le site service public le montant correspondant est de 36,37 € (https://www.service-public.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F2882). 

Cet écart me semble dû au fait que dans OpenFisca le montant forfaitaire de la PA (parameter.prestations.minima_sociaux.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa.yaml) est de 536,36 € alors qu'il est de 526,25 € selon service-public.
Par ailleurs, dans OpenFisca, le montant forfaitaire du RSA (parameter.prestations.minima_sociaux.rmi.rmi.yaml) n'est pas à jour : il est maintenu à 535,17 € depuis le 1er septembre 2016, alors qu'il a été revalorisé à 545,48 € le 1er septembre 2017 (source : https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/eli/decret/2017/5/4/AFSA1707122D/jo).

Pour corriger cette erreur, il me semble qu'il faut fournir un travail plus important qu'une simple mise à jour des deux paramètres sus-cités, si on veut que l'ensemble fonctionne bien pour toutes les années de législation déjà codées.

Ci-dessous, le cas-type que j'ai essayé de simuler :
# Initialize test case
scenario.init_single_entity(
    period = 2017,
# Variable describing the individuals
    parent1 = dict(
        age = 30,
        salaire_net = 1300*12, # Annual basis
        ),
 # Variable describing the entity
    menage = dict(loyer = 5000, # Annual basis
            statut_occupation_logement = 1,
            taxe_habitation = -600,
            ),
    )

issue_title: DTEND always shows up as occurrence
issue_body:
I think I'm misunderstanding how this all works, but for example I have a recurrence rule describing "Every Day" with a dtend of July 20th. I also have a exception date of July 20th.

Finding all occurrences for this pattern shows July 20th still present. I would have thought July 20th would be excluded.

Example script (note that July 20th is printed):
```python
from datetime import datetime
import recurrence

rule = recurrence.Rule(recurrence.DAILY)
pattern = recurrence.Recurrence(
    dtstart=datetime(2017, 6, 27, 0, 0, 0),
    dtend=datetime(2017, 7, 20, 0, 0, 0),
    rrules=[rule,],
    exdates=[
        datetime(2017, 1, 20, 0, 0, 0),
    ],
)

print(list(pattern.occurrences()))
```

Alternatively, and likely related, declaring a rule like "Every Sunday" and having a DTEND will still make DTEND come back when getting occurrences with `between()` even if that DTEND date doesn't fall within the declared rules.

Is this working as intended? And If so, what do I need to do have recurrence rules that have end dates?

If not working as intended I will attempt to fix.

issue_body:
Following this documentation to query from a table

https://google-cloud-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/bigquery/usage.html#run-a-dry-run-query

**sample code for method 2**

from google.cloud import bigquery

```

issue_title: Bigquery Jobs should match the Futures interface
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'c0c0c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': '8bc34a', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Presently, bigquery uses a custom [`Job`](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/job.py#L85) class (and subclasses) to deal with long-running operations such as queries and data import.

The Job class causes the proliferation of code like this around all of our samples and user code:

```python
def wait_for_job(job):
    while True:
        job.reload()  # Refreshes the state via a GET request.
        if job.state == 'DONE':
            if job.error_result:
                raise RuntimeError(job.errors)
            return
        time.sleep(1)
```

Which in terms means our samples require a level on indirection to accomplish simple tasks:

```python
client = bigquery.Client()
query_job = client.run_async_query(str(uuid.uuid4()), query)
query_job.use_legacy_sql = False
query_job.begin()

wait_for_job(query_job)

rows = query_job.results().fetch_data(max_results=10)
for row in rows:
    print(row)
```

However, our gax-based clients share a common "Long Running Operation" strategy that surfaces these types of on-going operations as a class that implements the [`concurrent.futures.Future`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/concurrent.futures.html#future-objects) interface. This is implemented as [`gax._OperationFuture`](https://github.com/googleapis/gax-python/blob/3d48d1fe3cb149f875328630267ce148565cbb35/google/gax/__init__.py#L549). bigquery unfortunately can not use this as it's an http-only API and gax can only currently be used for gRPC APIs.

We should make the `_BaseJob` class and its subclasses closely conform to the `Future` interface so that the usage is similar to other API client and so that we can simplify usage to:

```python
client = bigquery.Client()
query_job = client.run_async_query(str(uuid.uuid4()), query)
query_job.use_legacy_sql = False
query_job.begin()

# Wait for the job to finish
query_result = query.result()

rows = query_result.fetch_data(max_results=10)
for row in rows:
    print(row)

```

issue_title: Explore more efficient analysis format for pants internal usage
issue_body:
This is a follow up task for #3962 .

Analysis files are used by
* zinc itself for incremental compile
* pants for products and dependencies

Right now they share the same plain text file, but they don't have to.

zinc can use the zip file like upstream does, which may have performance benefit when files are large and many.

pants can use a more compact internal representation, this perhaps is necessary since we need a place for the reconstructed inheritance dependencies.

issue_title: Permissions set up incorrectly if path parameter in CloudFormation Lambda api event and api Swagger definition have different names
issue_body:
**Description:**

Lambda function defined as _AWS::Serverless::Function_ with an event of type _Api_ and a path parameter needlessly requires that the path parameter has the exact same name in Api Gateway definition. If the path parameter names are not identical, calls to Api Gateway result in the following error: _"Execution failed due to configuration error: Invalid permissions on Lambda function"_. SAM should be able to match the two parameter through their identical position in the path and setup permissions correctly, like it does for api events that do not use path parameters and for cases where the path parameters have the same name in both places.

This issue was discovered in a setup where CloudFormation scripts always use _{identifier}_ as path parameter name, so that retrieving the parameters could be done identically for multiple different api calls, while api definition use descriptive names in order to more clearly describe the purpose of the api.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Define an _AWS::Serverless::API_ using a Swagger file that contains a call with path parameter called _{identifier}_
2. Define an _AWS::Serverless::Function_ that has an _Event_ with _Type: Api_ and _Path: {another_identifier}_ and couple it to api of previous step with _RestApiId_ parameter.
2. Deploy the application
3. Invoke the call defined in the first step 1.

**Observed result:**
Error message _"Execution failed due to configuration error: Invalid permissions on Lambda function"_ is returned.

**Expected result:**
SAM and CloudFormation setup policy that allows Api Gateway to Invoke lambda. Api call is executed successfully.

**Workaround:**
Define function policy separately, e.g.
```
  ApiPermissions:
    Type: 'AWS::Lambda::Permission'
    DependsOn:
      - ExampleLambda
      - ExampleApiGateway
    Properties:
      Action: lambda:InvokeFunction
      FunctionName: !GetAtt ExampleLambda.Arn
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
```

**Example files:**

Swagger Test.yml:
```
---
swagger: '2.0'
x-amazon-apigateway-policy:
  Version: "2012-10-17"
  Statement:
    - Effect: "Allow"
      Principal: "*"
      Action: execute-api:Invoke
      Resource:
        - execute-api:/v1/*
info:
  title: Http Method Value Test
  version: 0.0.3
  description: Testing aws Serverless Application Model
host: api.example.com
basePath: /v1
schemes:
  - https
securityDefinitions:
  bearerAuth:
    in: header
    name: Authorization
    type: apiKey
security:
  - bearerAuth: []
paths:
  /{identifier}:
    get:
      summary: GET in parameterized path
      description: GET in parameterized path
      consumes:
        - application/json
      produces:
        - application/json
      parameters:
        - $ref: '#/parameters/identifier'
      x-amazon-apigateway-integration:
        httpMethod: post
        type: aws_proxy
        uri:
          Fn::Sub: arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${HttpMethodValueTestLambda.Arn}/invocations
      responses:
        200:
          $ref: '#/responses/standardResponse'
parameters:
  path_parameter:
    name: identifier
    in: path
    type: string
    required: true
    description: Test path parameter
responses:
  standardResponse:
    description: Standard test response
    schema:
      properties:
        status:
          $ref: '#/definitions/status'
definitions:
  status:
    type: string
    description: Test call result status
    enum:
      - success
      - failed
      - partial
```

CloudFormation:
```
---
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31

Globals:
  Function:
    Runtime: dotnetcore2.1
    Timeout: 60

Resources:
  HttpMethodValueTestLambda:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      CodeUri: ./publish
      Handler: Test::Test.Rest.LambdaHandler::ApiGatewayHandler
      Role: arn:aws:iam::206640028901:role/services/lambda/TestProcessQueueExecutionRole
      Events:
        GetById:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /{another_identifier}
            Method: GET
            RestApiId: !Ref HttpMethodValueTestApiGateway
  FunctionSecurityGroup:
    Type: "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup"
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: "HttpMethodValueTest - Lambda Function SG"
      VpcId: !ImportValue 'Dev-VPCId'
  HttpMethodValueTestApiGateway:
    Type: 'AWS::Serverless::Api'
    Properties:
      DefinitionBody:
        'Fn::Transform':
          Name: 'AWS::Include'
          Parameters:
            Location: !Sub >-
              s3://test/test/Test.yml
      StageName: v1
```

issue_title: Enrolling a large number (~10) of learners into a class causes an error
issue_labels: [{'id': 492822760, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20backend', 'name': 'DEV: backend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 492821376, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20frontend', 'name': 'DEV: frontend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 667034823, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/P1', 'name': 'P1', 'color': 'FFAB4A', 'default': False}, {'id': 447172036, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20bug', 'name': 'TASK: bug', 'color': '72486f', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Originally discussed in in #2073.

Batch enrolling Learners into a class creates 1 request per Learner. When enrolling multiple learners, this seems to cause a race condition where the database can be locked performing one transaction while another one is being attempted.

One proposed solution is to allow the relevant endpoint to accept many learners to add to a class, so only one request is made for the whole batch.

issue_title: set all interaction times on the server-side
issue_labels: [{'id': 492822760, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20backend', 'name': 'DEV: backend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 492821376, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20frontend', 'name': 'DEV: frontend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 492816641, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20tech%20update', 'name': 'TASK: tech update', 'color': '996189', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
In order to make sure that all times in a kolibri instance are comparable to each other, interaction time stamps should be generated only on the server.

Times should not be from the client to the server.

In order to fully implement this, it may be necessary to bootstrap a "current time" variable into the client and then save a "time offset" value for certain operations. For example, in coach reports we need to calculate "how long ago" something happened using server-provided dates and comparing them to current client time + offset.

See original discussion: https://trello.com/c/30e6945U/799-make-interaction-log-times-comparable

issue_title: High-resolution sampling
issue_body:
I was reading the paper https://arxiv.org/pdf/1712.03439.pdf mentioning that they sample the IR at 1024kHz, then resample to 128kHZ and do a low-pass filtering at 80kHz, then resample to the audio frequency of 16kHz

I have a few questions:

 - from their paper it seems that they seem to use 1024kHz sampling because of the distance between the microphones, would we expect to still have better results even in the single microphone case if we do a higher-resolution computation, then a resampling?

 - when I do the computation at 1024kHz and resample at 16kHz, the signal is not scaled in the same way, it seems that if I apply a scaling of `1024 / 16` I get comparable values for the `max`, although the resulting IR do not look the same (they seem slightly "shifted" in time). Is there a better way to do the resampling?

 - their paper shows how they do efficient OLA computations, is this what is used in `pyroomacoustics`, and would it require a lot of work to add it if it was needed?

issue_title: Ueberschall: netmap loop
issue_body:
While migrating the Ueberschall accelerator code here, I stumbled over some sketch code that is worth keeping back to some day.

```python
c = 0
stats = {}


def start(app):
    # set inbox/outbox to first configured RX/TX queue (eg from a NIC or an NVMe)
    app.inbox.append(app.rx[0].queue)
    app.outbox.append(app.tx[0].queue)

    # start looping with freq capped to 100 Hz (or 10 ms period)
    app.loop(100)

def loop(app):
    # process incoming message (mbuf) queued in inbox
    for msg in app.inbox:

        # msg is a mbuf instance
        n = len(msg)

        # stopping the app with error
        if n > 1000:
            app.stop(Exception(u'msg too long! stopping'.encode()))

        # loop can keep state from outer scope
        if n not in stats:
            stats[n] = 0
        stats[n] += 1

        # mbuf handling
        msg2 = msg.duplicate()
        msg2.write(20, b'hello')

        # queue outgoing message (mbuf) in outbox
        app.outbox.send(msg2)

        # stopping the app with success, returning a result
        if c > 10000:
            result = {u'total': c, u'stat': stats}
            app.stop(result)

def stop(app):
    # stop or restart the app
    if app.err:
        print('unexpected error - exciting', app.err)
    else:
        # restart the app
        app.start()
```

issue_title: Make Attention more memory efficient
issue_body:
The way I originally implemented the `Attention` module lets you swap out `SimilarityFunctions` to try a bunch of options if you want, but it does so at the expense of memory usage, often using _way_ more memory than is necessary.  It'd be better to just have `Attention` be an abstract, `Registrable` `Module`, with concrete implementations matching our `SimilarityFunctions`, but more memory efficient.  You'd still get the modularity, but without sacrificing memory usage.  #346 has one instance of this for `MatrixAttention`.

Next time I'm working on a model where this is the bottleneck, I'll just fix it, but I don't know when that will be.  If someone wants to contribute modules that do this, that'd be great.

issue_title: Order of dimensions in multichannel images
issue_body:
In PR https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/pull/118 and https://github.com/soft-matter/pims/pull/109, I started working on importing images with more than one channel (C), focal plane (Z) and time points (T). The dimensional order I've adopted is (TCZYX), starting with the outer dimension.

I've read https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/1280 and the scikit convention is to put multichannels in the last dimension. I have a strong tendency to go along with this convention, but it does raise a few issues:

- this dimensional order is unconventional in microscopy (except for RGB). For example, ImageJ, which is the most standard image analysis tools always has YX as last two dimensions.
- `trackpy` operates on grayscale images, so a conversion `process_func` is always necessary if you import a multichannel. For instance, subtracting two channels is less computer-intensive when channel is the first dimension. (compare `image[0] - image[1]` to `image[:,:,0] - image[:,:,1]`)
- `_repr_html_` in `frame.py` distinguishes multichannels from RGBs based on the location of multichannel axis. A 3-channel multichannel would be indistinguishable from RGB, unless we decide to give the `frame` object an `isRGB` property. Which I am in favour of.

Question is: do we keep it as it is or should we go along with scikit-image and put channels in the inner dimension? What is your opinion?

issue_title: Unexpected behavior with link_df and its memory argument when frames missing
issue_body:
Hi all,
I have found that the `memory` argument to `link_df()` is essentially ignored on a test case I made consisting of a single particle that disappears for a couple frames and then returns in **trackpy 0.3.0**. In the example below, the last appearance of the particle is erroneously grouped into the track for the first four particles, despite its appearance after a time longer than the `memory` argument.

```python
import pandas as pd
import trackpy as tp

test_df = pd.DataFrame({'x' : [10, 20, 0, 0, 20], 'y' : [10, 20, 20, 0, 0], 'frame' : [0, 1, 2, 3, 6]})

tp.link_df(test_df, 25, memory = 1)

assert test_df['particle'].max() >= 1, 'Error: all features grouped into one particle.'
```

An AssertionError will be raised because every element of the `test_df['particle']` column will be zero. However, if I add another particle such that the gap between frames 3 and 6 is filled, I get the expected results, where the last feature is not joined to the first four.

```python
import pandas as pd
import trackpy as tp

test_df = pd.DataFrame({'x' : [10, 20, 0, 100, 0, 110, 90, 90, 20, 110], 'y' : [10, 20, 20, 100, 0, 110, 110, 90, 0, 90], 'frame' : [0, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6]})

tp.link_df(test_df, 25, memory = 1)

assert test_df['particle'].max() == 2, 'Error: Unexpected number of one particles.'
```

The above will not raise the AssertionError.

issue_title: Add Unity Disk total latency and queue length support.
issue_body:
Add metric `total_latency` and `queue_length` for unity disk.
Required metrics:
* sp.\*.physical.disk.\*.busyTicks
* sp.\*.physical.disk.\*.sumArrivalQueueLength
* sp.\*.physical.disk.\*.reads
* sp.\*.physical.disk.\*.writes
* sp.\*.physical.coreCount

Formula:
* Total calls for each SP: 
  >delta(reads) + delta(writes)
* Total Latency for each SP: 
  >((delta(busyTicks) * (delta(sumArrivalQueueLength)/(total calls))) / (total calls)) / coreCount
* Total Latency: 
  >((spa total latency) * (spa total calls) + (spb total latency) * (spb total calls)) / ((spa total calls) + (spb total calls)) / 1000`
* Queue Length for each SP: 
  >(delta(sumArrivalQueueLength)/(total calls)) / coreCount
* Queue Length: 
  >((spa Queue Length) * (spa total calls) + (spb Queue Length) * (spb total calls)) / ((spa total calls) + (spb total calls))

issue_title: Conda / Aanconda-Server Integration Points
issue_body:
Here are some of the pain points of using conda 

1. Aanconda-Server provides a dynamic view on live changing user-data. The `repodata.json` is far from efficient in this case. This is the cause of most of the server CPU bottlenecks and db-server IO bottlenecks. It would be great if anaconda cluster could use the anaconda cluster RESTful API and assemple the repodata.json internally. this was demoed in kapsel and benchmarked a large performance gain when using multiple channels.
1. Errors messages - anaconda server provides json error messages that conda can use
1. Login - it would be great if conda and anconda could prompt or require login before `conda install` calls. for example in the `.condarc` a setting like `using_private_packages: yes` would prompt users to login before continuing.
1. Channel syntax. Conda is designed to be fully reproducible, however the number of channels on anconda.org makes this very difficult. A new syntax like `@sean/package` would allow installation of a specific package that could be installed without a user having to modify the global conda state and prevents ambiguous package resolutions. eg `conda install -c sean -c ben numpy salt` - what packages of numpy and salt will be installed in this scenario? That is currently undefined. This could be better written as  `conda install @sean/numpy @ben/salt`

issue_title: anaconda 4.4 update + RH5 = broken anaconda
issue_body:
Hello,
I update my anaconda2 4.4.0 based installation on a red hat 5.11, now it is broken because of the system GLIBC is too old:
```
/path/anaconda2-4.4.0/bin/python: /lib64/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.7' not found (required by /path/anaconda2-4.4.0/bin/../lib/libpython2.7.so.1.0)
```
It looks like the update pulled new packages from anaconda 5.0.0 through a new channel which is now 
`https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main`
instead of
`https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free`
The update procedure shall check the platform requirement before making those changes.

I then tried to run conda `update --all` from a brand new anaconda2 4.4.0 installation, everything seems to be working OK, but it looks like any new package I want to install will be pulled from the new main-based default channel.

How could I stay on the old default channel so I do not break my installation?

issue_title: Writing a VCF with no variants omits header (regression introduced in 0.10.0)
issue_body:
When writing out a VCF, if there are no variant rows written then the header is not written either. This is unexpected behavior that was introduced after 0.9.0 (which operates as expected) and is present in 0.10.0 and 0.11.0.

(In the example below `input.vcf.gz` is any sample VCF you have lying around)
```
import pysam

print(pysam.__version__)
vcf_in = pysam.VariantFile('input.vcf.gz', 'r')
vcf_out = pysam.VariantFile('test.vcf', 'w', header=vcf_in.header)
```
Running this with 0.9.0 produces the expected result

```
$ python pysam-vcf-bug.py 
0.9.0
$ cat test.vcf 
##fileformat=VCFv4.2
##FILTER=<ID=PASS,Description="All filters passed">
##fileDate=20160824
##contig=<ID=1,length=249250621>
##contig=<ID=2,length=243199373>
##contig=<ID=3,length=198022430>
##contig=<ID=4,length=191154276>
##contig=<ID=5,length=180915260>
##contig=<ID=6,length=171115067>
##contig=<ID=7,length=159138663>
##contig=<ID=8,length=146364022>
##contig=<ID=9,length=141213431>
##contig=<ID=10,length=135534747>
##contig=<ID=11,length=135006516>
##contig=<ID=12,length=133851895>
##contig=<ID=13,length=115169878>
##contig=<ID=14,length=107349540>
##contig=<ID=15,length=102531392>
##contig=<ID=16,length=90354753>
##contig=<ID=17,length=81195210>
##contig=<ID=18,length=78077248>
##contig=<ID=19,length=59128983>
##contig=<ID=20,length=63025520>
##contig=<ID=21,length=48129895>
##contig=<ID=22,length=51304566>
##contig=<ID=X,length=155270560>
##contig=<ID=Y,length=59373566>
##contig=<ID=MT,length=16569>
##FORMAT=<ID=GT,Number=1,Type=String,Description="Consensus Genotype across all datasets with called genotype">
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	ref
```

But for later versions of pysam, we get an empty file
```
$ python pysam-vcf-bug.py 
0.10.0 (or 0.11.0)
$ cat test.vcf 
$ 
```

Adding a `vcf_out.close()` statement results in a segfault on 0.10.0 but on 0.11.0 will write out the header.

Thanks!
-Kaushik

issue_title: VUnit fails if execute nested projects with GHDL

I have a problem in executing VUnit tests from nested project with GHDL.
Let's suppose that we have some VHDL project with the following structure:
```

├── lib1

├── lib2

│   └── tb_dff.vhd

```python
import glob
import os

from vunit import VUnit

prj_root = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
pattern = "*.vhd"

ui = VUnit.from_argv()

for root, dirs, _ in os.walk(prj_root):
    # Skip searching in hidden directories
    dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not d.startswith('.')]
    if glob.glob(os.path.join(root, pattern)):

issue_title: Curator should have a silent mode
issue_body:
When running curator as a cron job, it should be possible to have it produce nothing to stdout in normal operation. However, a command such as the following will cause the following output:

`root@server:~# curator --logfile /tmp/curator.log --loglevel ERROR delete indices --prefix logstash- --older-than 30 --time-unit days --timestring %Y.%m.%d`
**No indices matched provided args.**

There should be a --quiet option or similar.

issue_title: Add configuration validation
issue_body:
I am a new user to curator and with the latest version of curator (4.0.0) I started with the following action config:

```
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    fitlers:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: logstash-
    - filtertype: age
      source: name
      timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
      direction: older
      unit: days
      unit_count: 5
```

Instead of 'filters' i typed in 'fitlers', which is a fairly common error to make.

As a result, all my indices in elastics are removed. And it took me some time to figure out why this happened.

I would suggest that curator provide better protection against this sort of disaster. For example, when unrecognized options are encountered, an error shall be raised.

As in SQL, a statement like DELETE FROM table_name WHRER 1=1 shall not delete any thing.

A quick search with the term `HTTPSConnectionPool` among storage issues will come up with a dozen of issues caused by various problems. The term becomes ambiguous to the customer and hides the actual error message.

issue_title: 'Storage accounts update' has required field that should be optional
issue_body:
`storage_accounts.update` takes a `StorageAccountUpdateParameters` object.
This `StorageAccountUpdateParameters` object takes a `Sku` object.
`Sku` has a name field that is required.

This means to update a storage account, you **have** to set Sku.name which is not the case with the raw REST API.

I've marked this as an issues in the azure-rest-api-specs repo [#370](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/issues/370).

However, the issue should be tracked here also as the SDK may have to be updated was the swagger spec is updated also.

But this produces an error on Docker 1.11.2:

docker: "build" requires 1 argument.

The path needs to go at the end on 1.11.2, but it works on 1.13.0 as-is. We should probably just change the order of the arguments to make every version happy.

issue_title: Configuration v2
issue_body:
A bit of an RFC

## Issues

There's some "problems" with the current configuration and I'd like to address those in a backwards incompatible way (probably through a migration period where both formats are allowed).

There's two primary issues I'd like to address with the current configuration format:
- The top level is currently a list, making it difficult to add repository-level configuration: #240 #281 (as well as other things I'd like such as repository-level interpreter version)
- The configuration is not scriptably rewriteable: #210

There's also auxiliary issues:
- yaml is not maintained
- yaml has some security issues #396
- `hooks.yaml` isn't well namespaced

## Proposal

### A new configuration language

I shopped around for configuration languages which fit the criteria above, notably:
- Provides a non-C implementation
- Supports comments + maps + lists
- Supports round-trip rewritability
- Has a python implementaiton

And did not find anything.  Given that, I've started speccing and implementing [a configuration language](https://github.com/asottile/dumbconf) with those design goals in mind.  It also aims to be significantly simpler than yaml (the spec may not fit on a postcard like json, but it's also not 70+ pages like yaml).

### New filenames

The names of the files will need to change for two reasons:
- The configuration will no longer be yaml
- hooks.yaml is not a namespaced-enough name

Proposing:
- `.pre-commit-config` - describes the consumer configuration (previously `.pre-commit-config.yaml`)
- `.pre-commit-manifest` - describes the repository configuration (previously `hooks.yaml`)

### New configuration structure

The new configuration structure will be a top-level mapping type to allow repository-wide configuration.  This map will have a `hooks` key which will contain the current configuration.

```yaml
hooks: [
        ...,
]
```

This will allow for top-level configuration which will apply to all hooks (such as global excludes, interpreter versions, etc.)

## Migration

- The new configuration will be introduced.
- In that release and the next two releases (or the next 3 months, whichever comes sooner), pre-commit will load both configuration formats but issue warnings for the legacy format.
- After the migration period, the old configuration format will be purged.

The "officially" maintained repositories ([pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks) as well as the [mirrors-*](https://github.com/pre-commit) repositories) will force-push tags for the lastest $n versions and provide both configuration formats for those tags.  Going forward, [pre-commit/pre-commit-mirror-maker](pre-commit/pre-commit-mirror-maker) will only produce the new configuration format.

To assist in migration (though the formats should be relatively easy to manually migrate) a tool will be created `pre-commit-migrate` which will attempt to take the legacy format and produce new configuration files.  Given yaml's lack of rewritability this will likely reformat lists and drop comments, but for the last time!

Thoughts?

issue_title: httpsplitting that proxy_pass leads to can't be used
OverflowError when trying to call match on big data
issue_body:
When I try to call the `match` method, an OverflowException occurs. Here is a simple example : 

```python
import yara

# the capture size is 2.3G
with open('../captures/bg1.pcap', 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()

rule = yara.compile(source='rule foo: bar {strings: $a = "lmn" condition: $a}')
matches = rule.match(data=data)
```

Here is the result :

```bash
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 10, in <module>
    matches = rule.match(data=data)
OverflowError: size does not fit in an int
```

After reading the file `yara-python.c`, I saw that the problem comes from the call to the function `PyArg_ParseTupleAndKeywords` and more specifically from the format chunk `s#`. Indeed, as specified in the Python documentation (https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/arg.html) :

>  For all # variants of formats (s#, y#, etc.), the type of the length argument (int or Py_ssize_t) is controlled by defining the macro PY_SSIZE_T_CLEAN before including Python.h. If the macro was defined, length is a Py_ssize_t rather than an int. This behavior will change in a future Python version to only support Py_ssize_t and drop int support. It is best to always define PY_SSIZE_T_CLEAN.

In order to fix this problem, I'll propose a simple pull request (just define the macro).

issue_title: Operations taking CryptographicParameters fail if set to default None
issue_body:
If you call `encrypt` (or I think anything else taking a `cryptographic_parameters` argument) without specifying the `cryptographic_parameters`, an exception is thrown:

```client.encrypt(b"some data", uuid)```

raises

```
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/pie/client.py", line 1252, in _build_cryptographic_parameters
    raise TypeError("Cryptographic parameters must be a dictionary.")
```

The problem is `encrypt` checks for and accepts the default None, but it then passes its argument to `self._build_cryptographic_parameters` which accepts only a dict.

I think all that is required is to add something like

if value is None:
   value = {}

at the start of `_build_cryptographic_parameters`

issue_title: Support Python 3.7
issue_body:
*I'm moving https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/pull/90#issuecomment-301317699 here since we have GitHub issue support now.*

It looks like the [CPython 3.7 failure](https://travis-ci.org/PyCQA/pyflakes/jobs/231571260) is related to a change in the AST. The docstring is no longer in `node.body`.

https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/46

We can mostly replace our `getDocstring(node.body[0])` call with `node.docstring`. But I'm not sure how to get the line number of the docstring anymore. `node.lineno` is close, but not quite the line number of the docstring.

https://github.com/PyCQA/pyflakes/blob/074c21da3174bc88b2ec02fea767a436e2db643d/pyflakes/checker.py#L873

```diff
diff --git a/pyflakes/checker.py b/pyflakes/checker.py
index 382574e..2c19533 100644
--- a/pyflakes/checker.py
+++ b/pyflakes/checker.py
@@ -870,7 +870,11 @@ class Checker(object):

     def handleDoctests(self, node):
         try:
-            (docstring, node_lineno) = self.getDocstring(node.body[0])
+            if hasattr(node, 'docstring'):
+                docstring = node.docstring
+                node_lineno = ???  # TODO
+            else:
+                (docstring, node_lineno) = self.getDocstring(node.body[0])
             examples = docstring and self._getDoctestExamples(docstring)
         except (ValueError, IndexError):
             # e.g. line 6 of the docstring for <string> has inconsistent
```

I've reported this to the [CPython issue tracker](http://bugs.python.org/issue30497).

issue_title: "-a standalone run" fails confusingly without installer
issue_body:
There are no installers in the standard distribution apart from `null.Installer`, which is hidden from the UI. So, if you remove `letsencrypt-apache` and `letsencrypt-nginx`, `letsencrypt -a standalone` (or more explicitly `letsencrypt -a standalone run`) will fail to find a candidate plugin for installer without any sensible warnings. This is behavior is confusing. Maybe default verb should be `auth` if no installers are available?

cc: @hlieberman

issue_title: `revoke` doesn't output any status
issue_body:
If I revoke:

	$ ./letsencrypt-auto revoke --cert-path=/path/to/cert1.pem --key-path=/path/to/privkey1.pem

This is the output:	

	Checking for new version...
	Requesting root privileges to run letsencrypt...
	   sudo /Users/jvanasco/.local/share/letsencrypt/bin/letsencrypt revoke --cert-path=/path/to/cert1.pem --key-path=/path/to/privkey1.pem

Did it work?  Let's try it again.

	$ ./letsencrypt-auto revoke --cert-path=/path/to/cert1.pem --key-path=/path/to/privkey1.pem

An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: Certificate already revoked
Please see the logfiles in /var/log/letsencrypt for more details.

The log is not "human readable" when it comes to figuring out if there was a success or not.  I can parse the info, I'm pretty sure most people can't.

This command really needs to output a success message.

issue_title: Incorrect Apache config generated for TLS challenge on IPv6 host
issue_body:
See https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/please-add-ipv6-support-to-the-client/12006

```
After doing some debugging, it turned out that the client is not supporting IPv6. It writes a file called /etc/apache2/le_tls_sni_01_cert_challenge.conf with contains the following lines:

<VirtualHost 46.4.113.142:443 [2a01:443>
...
<VirtualHost [2a01:443>
```

issue_title: Help text for -d (--domains) command line flag and the plural form of the long name
issue_body:
Seems like the short help isn't descriptive enough to point the user to the right direction when using multiple domain names, as seen here #1383 . 

Also the plural form of the long name is a bit deceptive, as it actually takes only one domain as an argument.

issue_title: renew --force-renew attempts to install test certs
issue_body:
using `letsencrypt-auto renew --force-renew` gave me the following error:

```
2016-03-01 07:34:57,850:WARNING:letsencrypt.cli:Attempting to renew cert from 
/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/[...].conf produced an unexpected error: You've asked to renew/replace a
seemingly valid certificate with a test certificate (domains: [...]). We will not do that unless you use
the --break-my-certs flag!. Skipping.
```

renewing the "old way", 

```
/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto certonly --webroot -w [...] -d [...] --force-renewal --non-interactive
```

worked fine.  [edit: formatting]

issue_title: Cannot connect to a server that is only binding 'localhost'
issue_body:
In development, my local Kafka broker only binds `localhost`:

```
listeners=PLAINTEXT://localhost:9093
```

If I use the "High-level Consumer" code example code from [the documentation](https://github.com/confluentinc/confluent-kafka-python#usage), the consumer fails to connect to my broker:

```
%3|1502723013.325|FAIL|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:localhost:9093/bootstrap]: localhost:9093/bootstrap: Connect to ipv6#[::1]:9093 failed: Connection refused
%3|1502723013.325|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:localhost:9093/bootstrap]: localhost:9093/bootstrap: Connect to ipv6#[::1]:9093 failed: Connection refused
%3|1502723013.325|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-1| [thrd:localhost:9093/bootstrap]: 1/1 brokers are down
```

This seems to be because this consumer resolves `localhost` to an IPv6 address, but the broker doesn't bind this address.

A Java consumer doesn't have this problem.

Tested with Kafka 0.10 and 0.11.

+    res = ""
+    if isinstance(obj_,int):

+        return res
+    elif isinstance(obj_,str):
+        res += "%s" % obj_
+        return res
+    elif isinstance(obj_,dict):
+        res += ""
+        for k,v in obj_.iteritems():

+            member["name"]=k
+            member["value"]=from_obj_to_xmlrpc(v)

+    elif isinstance(obj_,list):
+        res += ""
+        for i in obj_:
+            res += "%s" % from_obj_to_xmlrpc(i)
+        res += ""
+        return res
+    else:

issue_title: QueryDict on resource.build_filters
issue_body:
Hi,

It's not clear if filters parameter on resource.build_filters receives a Dict or a [QueryDict](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.10/ref/request-response/#querydict-objects) type object. This is a problem when expecting filters to be a QueryDict (as it was on 0.13.1) and calling methods not in Dict.

Currently (from 0.13.2 onwards), it receives a QueryDict object when called from [resource.obj_get_list](https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/blob/66bf6331dede81753d3b2bbdc837f3af890d04dc/tastypie/resources.py#L2119) and a Dict object when called from [resource.obj_get](https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/blob/66bf6331dede81753d3b2bbdc837f3af890d04dc/tastypie/resources.py#L2138). Before, build_filters was never called on obj_get, this was changed on this commit https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/commit/66bf6331dede81753d3b2bbdc837f3af890d04dc

Tests are "inconsistent" as they test the function [with both types](https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/blob/master/tests/core/tests/resources.py) as if they were interchangeable.

What should build_filters receive? I can work on a PR if this is considered to be a general issue.

issue_title: Need to set spark.speculation=false when train model on BigDL
issue_body:
If spark.speculation=true, error will raise

```
java.lang.IllegalStateException: Please initialize AllReduceParameter first!
2017-07-28 18:26:09      at com.intel.analytics.bigdl.parameters.AllReduceParameter$$anonfun$readGradientPartition$2.apply(AllReduceParameter.scala:124)
2017-07-28 18:26:09      at com.intel.analytics.bigdl.parameters.AllReduceParameter$$anonfun$readGradientPartition$2.apply(AllReduceParameter.scala:124)
```

The root cause is Spark schedule "grad computing and grad accumulates" tasks to different executors. So the second task can't find the gradient block from the first task.

User can by pass this by adding ```--conf "spark.speculation=false"``` to the spark summit command

issue_title: Leaky Rectifier activation upcasts to float64
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed that the leaky rectifier activation upcasts the gradient of the weights in the layer. 
```
UserWarning: You are creating a TensorVariable with float64 dtype. You requested an action via the Theano flag warn_float64={ignore,warn,raise,pdb}.
  return theano.tensor.nnet.relu(x, self.leakiness)
```
I think that is related to #402 and that was closed on #661. So I just added this to the call:
```return theano.tensor.nnet.relu(x, theano.tensor.constant(self.leakiness))```

Should that be enough? Or should I also report it to Theano repo?

Thanks,
HM

issue_title: Encryption option for makedir()
issue_body:
Hello!

Would it be possible to extend the current support for encryption options to makedir() in the S3FS class?

I was excited to be able to use upload_args to pass server side encryption options for file uploads to an s3 bucket. It works fine for file uploads. However, I am unable to create a directory in an s3 bucket that has an encryption policy. Looking at the code, I think that the upload_args are not passed to s3 in makedir() as they are when files are created. I know a directory is essentially a dummy file as far as s3 is concerned but, as I am using PyFilesystem to leverage the abstraction, I'd like to continue to use makedir() in my code (i.e. to make it easier to swap different storage backends in and out). 

Thanks for the consideration!

issue_title: Sources - maintain the order as defined by input
issue_body:
We should keep the same order for files as it was defined by an input.

For instance
```
sources:
  group:
 - a
 - b
 - c
```

The generated project should show virtual folder group, and files a, b, c (same order as defined). This is currently not case.

issue_title: request: enumerated types
issue_body:
## Feature Request
I would like to store categorical data like cell type. It seems to be like the most natural way to store this in hdf5 would be enumerated data types. Oddly, enumerated data types are not supported in numpy, but are supported in hdf5. I'd like the ability to define an extension that expects an enumerated data type.

issue_title: Repr of djmoney.money.Money rounded down
issue_body:
Sorry if this has already been brought up, but I couldn't see anything obvious when searching for repr.

Within a console, if I do the following I get confusing results:

```py
In [63]: m
Out[63]: 9 GBP

In [64]: type(m)
Out[64]: djmoney.money.Money

In [65]: vars(m)
Out[65]: 
{'_constructor_args': ((), {'amount': Decimal('9.75'), 'currency': u'GBP'}),
 'amount': Decimal('9.75'),
 'currency': GBP}
```

However, constructing a `moneyed.Money` result produces the following:

```
In [67]: m2
Out[67]: <Money: 9.75 GBP>

In [68]: vars(m2)
Out[68]: {'amount': Decimal('9.75'), 'currency': GBP}

In [70]: type(m2)
Out[70]: moneyed.classes.Money
```
It seems like the [py-moneyed team took the decision to remove the rounding down in 2015](https://github.com/limist/py-moneyed/pull/67) - is there a reason you've chosen not to? Looking at your implementation would suggest that you copied the code verbatim when constructing your overloaded version. Should I be exclusively using `djmoney.money.Money` over `moneyed.Money`?

issue_title: Switch @nteract/core/constants to @nteract/core/actionTypes
issue_body:
We've got several different kinds of constants we use in our app currently:

* action types
* jupyter message types
* zeromq channels

To make things a bit more clear, we should adopt `actionTypes` as the name for the constants, which means we'd have `@nteract/core/actionTypes`.

issue_title: Problem with data migrations
issue_body:
Hi,
Imagine we have a model `class MyModel(TranslationMixin, Page)` with field 'description' and migrate the database. Then, we create a migration that creates a new instance of that class and run the migration. No problems so far. After this, we add a new field to the class 'name' and migrate the database.
Now the problem is when we recreate the database and run migrations, django runs the data migration and it gives an error saying the field 'name' does not exists.

In the code of 'models.py' of wagtail_modeltranslation there is a 'if' at the start of TranslationMixin __init__ that solves this problem, but that is not a good solution because when creating a page, wagtail_modeltranslation will not patch the fields for all languages.

Anyone has any ideias of how to solve this problem?

`odo("data/myfile.csv", "postgresql://user:password@some_ip:54322::tbl")`, where `user`, `password`, `some_ip` and `tbl` have the right values, I got the following:


```
...
OperationalError: (psycopg2.OperationalError) could not open file "/home/nanounanue/proyectos/test-project/data/myfile.csv" for reading: No such file or directory
 [SQL:  '\n            COPY trips FROM %(path)s ...
```

I noted two things with this error:

1. First `odo` is converting my  relative path to an absolute path  using (I guess) the information of my current directory (maybe with `os`?)

2. `odo` is using `COPY`  instead of `\copy` this means that the file must be in the server disk

So I copied the file to the server (to the directory `/tmp/raw-data/`), and  I tried again:


`odo("/tmp/raw-data/myfile.csv", "postgresql://user:password@some_ip:54322::tbl")`
and I got 

```
....
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/raw-data/2013-08-Citi-Bike-trip-data.csv'
```

(NOTE that my `raw-data` doesn' t exist in my local machine) 


This not work either.

Then, finally I created in my local machine the directory `/tmp/raw-data` and copied the file there. Then everything works. So, my guess is that `odo` is mixing the paths, I mean, it is creating the path from the local machine, even when he is trying to load from the remote machine...

This smells like a bug...

issue_title: DKPY2:  autopilot baudrate locked to 115200, should be configurable
issue_body:
In DroneKit Python ver. 2 (DKPY2), the connect() function uses mavutil's mavlink_connection function to open a serial connection to the autopilot.  The issue is that connect's call to mavlink_connection doesn't specify a baudrate and, therefore, mavlink_connection just uses its default baudrate of 115200.  If I'm not mistaken, this means there is no way to change the baudrate of the connection between a Companion Computer and an autopilot if you're using DKPY2.  It would be better if connect() added an input argument of baudrate=115200 that it relays to mavlink_connection, to allow for using different baudrates with your autopilot. 

    def connect(**ip**, await_params=False, status_printer=errprinter, vehicle_class=Vehicle, rate=4):
        state = MPFakeState(**mavutil.mavlink_connection(ip)**, vehicle_class=vehicle_class)
        state.status_printer = status_printer
        state.prepare(await_params=await_params, rate=rate)
        return state.vehicle

issue_title: R200 fails with librealsense after firmware upgrade
issue_body:
RS200 / Windows 10 / VS2013 

I've been having a few problems getting the R200 to work properly with LibRealsense. Even in the examples the camera would sometimes fail, throwing exceptions in the lib and requiring to be replugged to make it work again. It generally works, the CPPCapture sample shows the colour, depth and infrared images, but it doesn't feel stable at all. The same problems occur in the integration I've done with our software.

So I installed the latest DCM - Build Number: 2.1.24.9476 - and let it upgrade the firmware. And then the problems really started.. in LibRealsense it either doesn't recognise the camera at all, recognises the streams but hangs on the first attempt to get a buffer, or (best of all) bluescreens the PC when it tries to get a buffer.
Worse, it seems like that upgrade is a one way street. I can't find any way to uninstall the DCM and roll back to an older one, or any way to get back to the state I had before when the camera at least did something. 

Any advice much appreciated. We're integrating the cameras with our software (to go alongside Kinect1 and Kinect2) - but this is currently looking like a dead end.

issue_title: A2C does not normalize either advantage estimate or returns ? -> Loss explosion
issue_body:
I am benchmarking OpenAI baselines implementation of A2C in Acrobot-v1. There is a phenomenon 
 observed, because baselines original implementation does not normalize either advantage estimate before backprop the action head or normalize returns before backprop value head.

When testing with Acrobot-v1, it leads to loss explosion, (loss value becomes very large like 20000), then the policy cannot learn anything.

After adding normalization to the returns for the value head, the problem solved. 

So it seems the point is that when we allow large max time step for an episode, without normalization, the MSE loss with become very big ?

issue_title: Required mutation input arguments
Assertions unsafe in production code
issue_body:
I've noticed while reading graphene and graphene-django that assertions are used frequently to protect from operations with undefined results or things that should be impossible (but might still happen if used incorrectly).

In Python, assertions are not safe to use for this in general. This is because they can be disabled, and often are in production.

Instead of:

```python
assert something is not None, "Something was unexpectedly None"
```

We should do this:

```python
if something is not None:
    raise AssertionError("Something was unexpectedly None")
```

This is the recommended best practice in Python development where assertions are being used.

issue_title: Possible Bug: Output fields from DjangoFormMutation
issue_body:
In the [initialization](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/06ca766c3852ba2bf815fc615727244fcbe00671/graphene_django/forms/mutation.py#L103) of the Meta class of a DjangoFormMutation, the output fields are declared similar to the input fields of the mutation, like:
```python
input_fields = fields_for_form(form, only_fields, exclude_fields)
output_fields = fields_for_form(form, only_fields, exclude_fields)
```

For example, if we have a form for authentication, like the one provided by django:
```python
class AuthenticationForm(forms.Form):
    """
    Base class for authenticating users. Extend this to get a form that accepts
    username/password logins.
    """
    username = UsernameField(
        max_length=254,
        widget=forms.TextInput(attrs={'autofocus': True}),
    )
    password = forms.CharField(
        label=_("Password"),
        strip=False,
        widget=forms.PasswordInput,
    )
    ...
```
And we link it to a mutation:
```python
class AuthMutation(DjangoFormMutation):
    """
    Mutation to login a user
    """
    class Meta:
        form_class = UserLoginForm
    ...
```
generates a mutation that requires a username and a password on the response. 
```graphql
AuthMutationPayload{
    username: String!
    password: String!
    clientMutationId: String
}
```
Is this right? Is sending back the password to the user secure? I think the output field should be initialized as an `OrderedDict()`.

issue_title: Drop of packet due to invalidated neighbours
issue_body:
When I run some test on Windows, my ping function fails. Usually after number of nodes is ~20 or more. They are all in router states, and they're neighbours of one another.
After debugging a bit, it appeared that the reason of failure lies in `Mac::ReceiveDoneTask` here:
```
if (neighbor == NULL)
{
    otLogDebgMac("drop not neighbor");                         <<===== HERE
    ExitNow(error = kThreadError_UnknownNeighbor);
}
```
When I looked into why neighbor is NULL, I found out that `this->mNetif->mMleRouter->mRouters` has all of its records (aka neighbor routers) in an invalid state, including the sender of a packet.
It seems to me like the neighbour timeout has expired and they were invalidated. But I don't know why weren't they queried again or something.
Also, when I query neighbour using the node API, it returns that they are neighbours.
And unfortunately as for now this is all I've discovered about this issue, because this 'invalidation' happens to a random node, and before that happens it's not clear which one will fail, hence catch the moment when the neighbours are invalidated is very hard.

I was wondering if you would probably know what might be the issue, or if you could give me some pointers on where to look and what to check?

issue_title: Diagnostic commands
issue_body:
Currently OpenThread supports some of diagnostic commands (DIAG_GET.req, DIAG_GET.rsp and DIAG_RST.nrf). CLI example exposes command `networkdiagnostic get`, however a response is not populated back (see `NetworkDiagnostic::HandleDiagnosticGetResponse`).

I would like to get those information on CLI at least in raw tlv ASCII format e.g.: 
`DIAG_GET.ans: 0102fc00`
So the Openthread public API callback could be like: `otSetDiagnosticGetCallback(otInstance, callback(otMessage))`

Other option could be to parse DIAG_GET.rsp in the `NetworkDiagnostic::HandleDiagnosticGetResponse` and return some kind of structure (but who should allocate the memory for it, since DIAG_GET.ans consists of different number of members e.g. could carry 4 IPv6 addresses and 3 child information etc.) - however it may be challenging taking to account the amount of possible Diagnostic TLVs.

So generally there are two questions:
1. How do you want to Diagnostic Get callback to look like in Openthread API (return only otMessage and otMessageInfo or parsed structure etc.)
2. How to show responses on the CLI? Is it acceptable to print only raw payload in ASCII?

Also as a side task it might be usuful to implement two left commands DIAG_GET.qry and DIAG_GET.ans which are almost the same as already implemented with a difference that they are better for multicast queries due to different transaction pattern.

issue_title: ImportError: undefined symbol: _ZTIN4base16classE
issue_body:
I am working on a large library and trying to make it accessible from python. I want to be able to call functions in a derived class (and some setters in its parent class). The derived class is Class C. Class C inherits from Class B (an abstract class with one pure virtual method that also contains some setters I wish to call). Class B then inherits from Class A, which in turn inherits from boost::enable_shared_from_this\<A\>. 

I have written the module initialization code as follows.
```
    py::class_<C> (m, "C")
        .def(py::init<>());
```

However, when I do this the code compiles but I receive an "ImportError: undefined symbol: _ZTIN4base16classE" error when I try importing the module. (The real error is slightly different because the base class is not really called "baseClass".) It is telling me that Class B is not defined. I then tried to write the module initialization code as 
```
    py::class_<B> (m, "B")
        .def(py::init<>());

    py::class_<C, B> (m, "C")
        .def(py::init<>());
```

Yet, I still receive the same ImportError. Also, Class B is an abstract class. I am hoping that I do not need to expose it (or its parent Class A). I know I could keep trying to define Class B and Class A until this ImportError goes away, but I do not want to do that if it is not necessary. More importantly than the fact that it would be a lot of extra work, the pybind wrapper code would then become much harder to maintain. So I have a couple questions:

1) Must I write pybind code to expose the base classes?

2) Will I have to expose Class A in addition to Class B?

Thank you in advance for taking the time to help me out here.

issue_title: No transpose option for Eigen arguments/return values
issue_body:
Background:
To store a block of vertices, it is usual to use a Nx3 matrix in row major in numpy and a 3xN matrix in column major in C++ (Eigen). These 2 matrices are the same thing in memory, but pybind11 will automatically transpose in memory my Nx3 row major matrix in numpy to a Nx3 column matrix. I would like to keep my vertices in column major in C++

One way to implement this no transpose option for return value is to add additional enum value to `return_value_policy`. However, since `return_value_policy` values are not flags, if I am not mistaken, a `_notranspose` version for each enum is need.

For arguments, I can just add a notranspose option to `arg`

My questions are
* Am I missing something? Is there an easy way to do this?
* If no, would such a contribution be welcomed? I need this for my project, so I think I will implement this anyways.

issue_title: Monitor Channels & Dispute
Safe WAL replay between Raiden versions
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Suppose the following scenario:

- There is a Raiden node running on version 0.1
- Raiden releases a new version that introduced changes to the structure of some objects, e.g. StateChanges, these changes might either add or remove fields.
- The node running Raiden version 0.1 at some point upgrades to the new version, stopping the current running instance and starting a new one with the new version.

On restart, the new version must read the latest snapshot and unapplied state changes to recover the running state. If proper care is not taken, because of the changes deserialization might fail, if the deserialization works field might be missing and the task code fails.

## Proposed solution

- Version the state task objects (StateChange and State):
- Version the state task code
- Enforce upgradability rules:
  - Changes that add fields must follow with a new version of the task code.
  - Changes that removed fields /may/ upgrade the code in-place.
  - Renaming a field is the same as a remove + add, so the rules above apply.
  - Changing the type of a field is the same as a remove + add.

## Alternative approach

Instead of versioning the data structures *and* the code, it's possible to version *just* the data structures and use routines to upgrade the data. The drawback is that when a new field is added a default value must be used, and not always there will be reasonable defaults to choose from.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Start the versioning for the current code base.

issue_title: Allow 20% less precision option to drop some final 1, 2, 8, 9
issue_body:
Least significant digit that is equal to length precision can be safely rounded up or down if the digit is 1, 2, 8, 9. This is equivalent to 20% less precision so --set-precision 4 would behave like --set-precision 3.8

So --set-precision 3
3.593 is converted to 3.59, but its safe to round to 3.6
3.993 is converted to 3.99, but its safe to round to 4

This is not these same as --set-precision 2
3.593 and 3.553 both converted to 3.6 
3.993 is converted to 3.953 both converted to 4

This actually helps shave off a few more bytes/KB for large svgs, when the difference between when going from --precision 4 to --precision 3 is visually not acceptable.
 
Right now i do this manually as an additional step in a bash script outside of scour, but I feel others might find it helpful to integrate to their projects. 

I can submit a PR to demonstrate

issue_title: Better support for copying fields to sub factory - expose _Resolver__declarations.contexts
issue_body:
The solutions given in [the docs on copying fields to a subfactory](http://factoryboy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/recipes.html#copying-fields-to-a-subfactory) don't work properly.

Take the following factory:

```py
# students and clients both have a "user" field, students and clients should always share
# the same company defined at user.company
import factory.django
from models import Student
from factories import UserFactory, ClientFactory

gen_user = factory.LazyAttribute(lambda o: UserFactory(company=o.factory_parent.user.company))


class StudentFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory):
    class Meta:
        model = Student

    user = factory.SubFactory(UserFactory)
    # client = factory.SubFactory(ClientFactory, user=gen_user)

    @factory.lazy_attribute
    def client(self):
        kwargs = dict(user__company=self.user.company)
        kwargs.update(self._Resolver__declarations.contexts.get('client', {}))
        return ClientFactory(**kwargs)
```

The commented out solution `# client = factory.SubFactory(ClientFactory, user=gen_user)` doesn't work because if I define `StudentFactory(client__age=10, client__user__name='foobar')` `client.age` is honored but `client.user.name` is ignored.

The second solution with a `lazy_attribute` works but is ugly and wasn't easy to find.

`self._Resolver__declarations.contexts` should be exposed in a more user friendly variable and documented.

issue_title: VGG output layer must be softmax
issue_body:
I am still a beginner with pytorch, but it seems to me that the classifier part of the VGG model should have one extra line:

        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(512 * 7 * 7, 4096),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.Dropout(),
            nn.Linear(4096, 4096),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.Dropout(),
            nn.Linear(4096, num_classes),
            **nn.Softmax()**

       )
From the paper:
"A stack of convolutional layers (which has a different depth in different architectures) is followed by
three Fully-Connected (FC) layers: the first two have 4096 channels each, the third performs 1000-
way ILSVRC classification and thus contains 1000 channels (one for each class). **The final layer is
the soft-max layer**. The configuration of the fully connected layers is the same in all networks."

<!--
General questions should be asked on the mailing list ray-dev@googlegroups.com.

Before submitting an issue, please fill out the following form.

Ubuntu 16.04

pip installed from Linux 3.5 wheel
- **Ray version**:
0.5.0
- **Python version**:
3.5.5

compute_action(self, observation, state=None, policy_id="default")

<!--
You can obtain the Ray version with

python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
-->

### Describe the problem
<!-- Describe the problem clearly here. -->
agent.compute_action requires a 'state' argument which is the hidden state of the RNN. The docstring says it is supposed to return the RNN state along with the action, but currently it only returns the action. There is also no way to obtain the initial state.

### Source code / logs
<!-- Include any logs or source code that would be helpful to diagnose the problem. If including tracebacks, please include the full traceback. Large logs and files should be attached. Try to provide a reproducible test case that is the bare minimum necessary to generate the problem. -->

I am essentially running the following code:
```
lstm_state = [np.zeros(256), np.zeros(256)] # can't get initial state of RNN, so I initialize to 0 based on the shape wanted in the stack trace
action, lstm_state = agent.compute_action(state, lstm_state) # state is the current obs of the environment, this errors out saying 'np.float64 is not iterable', in other words it only returns the action value
```

issue_body:
The [_CLI usage_ documentation](http://python-gitlab.readthedocs.io/en/stable/cli.html#configuration) provides the following `.python-gitlab.cfg` file example:

```
[global]
...
api_version = 3

[somewhere]
...
api_version = 4

...
```
However, the `api_version` option in the `[global]` section seems having no effect.

with `[global] api_version = 3`:
```
user@machine:~$ gitlab -d project list
...
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https:/<host>:443 "GET /api/v3/projects HTTP/1.1" 200 None
...
```

with `[global] api_version = 4`: the result is the same, it uses v3 endpoints


FYI:
```
user@machine:~$ pip freeze | grep gitlab
python-gitlab==1.2.0
```

issue_body:
I'll start adding type hints to the source code starting from the re-write (#70)

Here will be my proposed approach:
1. Add python3 type things gradually: `def func(x: int) -> float` 
2. Add `mypy` to CI checks
3. For python2, we will _not_ use the `#type x: int -> float` comment workaround, instead when we distribute the package, we will strip away all the type hints [using strip-hints](https://pypi.org/project/strip-hints/) so the code is python2 compatible. 

Any comments and suggestions welcomed!

issue_title: Authorization URI should be accessible via authorize_url method
issue_body:
```authorize_url``` is currently an attribute on the Client class. It should be accessible via a method call:

```client = soundcloud.Client(auth_token='foo')
    client.authorize_url()```

Instead of 

```client = soundcloud.Client(auth_token='foo')
    client.authorize_url```

In the interest of backwards compatibility and robustness, perhaps it should be both.
